sources.name: The World According to GaWC 2024
sources.publisher: Globalization and World Cities Research Network, Loughborough University

sources.name: Numbeo Cost of Living, Property, Health Care & Crime Indices

cities.name: Amsterdam
cities.blurb: Highly livable and expat-friendly, but largely below sea level — flood defense is existential.

cities.name: Athens
cities.blurb: Sunny and affordable with tax breaks for newcomers; severe heat, drought and wildfire.

cities.name: Auckland
cities.blurb: Mild oceanic climate and political stability make it a classic climate haven — at a distance from everywhere.

cities.name: Austin
cities.blurb: No state income tax and strong job market, but fast-rising heat, drought and flash-flood risk.

cities.name: Bangkok
cities.blurb: Affordable, well-connected hub, but hot, low-lying and sinking — among the most flood-exposed cities here.

cities.name: Barcelona
cities.blurb: Mediterranean magnet with rising heat, drought and water stress on a crowded coast.

cities.name: Bengaluru
cities.blurb: India’s tech hub with a milder highland climate, but acute water stress and urban flooding.

cities.name: Berlin
cities.blurb: Temperate and water-secure for now, with gradually rising heat and drought summers.

cities.name: Bogotá
cities.blurb: High-altitude, mild capital; low heat but landslide, flooding and water variability.

cities.name: Boston
cities.blurb: Northern, water-secure and world-class for healthcare; coastal flooding is the main risk.

cities.name: Brussels
cities.blurb: The EU’s administrative capital; mild, temperate and low-hazard.

cities.name: Buenos Aires
cities.blurb: Temperate, affordable and culturally deep, shadowed by economic and political volatility.

cities.name: Cape Town
cities.blurb: Stunning and affordable, but a "Day Zero" water city with mounting drought and wildfire risk.

cities.name: Chicago
cities.blurb: Great-Lakes water security and a frequent climate-haven pick; cold, with rising heat and flooding.

cities.name: Copenhagen
cities.blurb: Cool, stable and well-governed; coastal and cloudburst flooding are the main climate watch-items.

cities.name: Doha
cities.blurb: Tax-free Gulf hub; extreme heat and water stress dominate, other hazards low.

cities.name: Dubai
cities.blurb: Tax-free global hub, but among the most heat- and water-stressed cities on the list.

cities.name: Dublin
cities.blurb: Mild, wet and water-secure with strong stability; coastal and rainfall flooding are the main risks.

cities.name: Frankfurt
cities.blurb: Europe’s financial and aviation hub; temperate with moderate, manageable climate risk.

cities.name: Helsinki
cities.blurb: Cool, stable and water-rich northern capital with very low climate hazard.

cities.name: Ho Chi Minh City
cities.blurb: Fast-growing Mekong-delta megacity that is sinking — highly flood- and heat-exposed.

cities.name: Hong Kong
cities.blurb: Dense, low-tax financial gateway; rising heat, humidity and typhoon-driven flooding.

cities.name: Istanbul
cities.blurb: Transcontinental megacity; earthquake risk layered on heat, drought and water stress.

cities.name: Jakarta
cities.blurb: A sinking capital — among the most flood- and subsidence-threatened cities on Earth.

cities.name: Kuala Lumpur
cities.blurb: Low-cost, English-capable tropical hub with easy long-stay visas; heat and flash floods are the trade-off.

cities.name: Lisbon
cities.blurb: Atlantic-tempered capital with mild winters, but a drying, fire-prone Iberian summer ahead.

cities.name: London
cities.blurb: Top-tier global hub with mild, water-secure climate; main exposure is tidal and surface flooding.

cities.name: Los Angeles
cities.blurb: Sprawling Pacific metro facing wildfire, drought, heat and chronic water stress.

cities.name: Madrid
cities.blurb: Inland capital facing intensifying heat and water stress, balanced by deep amenities.

cities.name: Medellín
cities.blurb: The "city of eternal spring" — mild year-round and inexpensive, with landslide and safety caveats.

cities.name: Melbourne
cities.blurb: Livable and stable, but exposed to heatwaves, drought cycles and regional bushfire smoke.

cities.name: Mexico City
cities.blurb: High-altitude mild temperatures offset by an acute, worsening water crisis and subsidence.

cities.name: Miami
cities.blurb: No state income tax — but ground zero for sea-level rise, hurricanes and extreme heat.

cities.name: Milan
cities.blurb: Italy’s business capital with a flat-tax regime for newcomers; Po Valley heat, drought and smog.

cities.name: Montevideo
cities.blurb: Stable, temperate Atlantic capital with an easy residency path and modest climate exposure.

cities.name: Mumbai
cities.blurb: India’s financial capital; extreme monsoon flooding, heat and sea-level exposure.

cities.name: Munich
cities.blurb: Prosperous, safe and alpine-adjacent; moderate, manageable climate exposure.

cities.name: New York
cities.blurb: The benchmark global city, with serious coastal-storm and flood exposure on a warming Atlantic.

cities.name: Oslo
cities.blurb: Wealthy, cool and water-secure; among the lowest-hazard cities here, but costly.

cities.name: Panama City
cities.blurb: Territorial-tax hub of the Americas with easy residency; tropical heat and flooding.

cities.name: Paris
cities.blurb: Dense cultural capital facing sharper heatwaves, with moderate Seine flood risk.

cities.name: Prague
cities.blurb: Affordable, safe central-European capital; temperate with low overall hazard.

cities.name: Rome
cities.blurb: Historic capital with a flat-tax option; Mediterranean heat, drought and water stress.

cities.name: San Francisco
cities.blurb: Tech capital with mild temperatures, but wildfire smoke, drought and bay sea-level rise.

cities.name: Santiago
cities.blurb: Stable and modern by regional standards, but locked in a long megadrought with heavy water stress.

cities.name: São Paulo
cities.blurb: Latin America’s economic engine; heat, recurring water crises and urban flooding.

cities.name: Seoul
cities.blurb: Tech-forward megacity with excellent healthcare; hot humid summers and monsoon flooding.

cities.name: Shanghai
cities.blurb: China’s commercial capital on a sinking coastal delta — flood, subsidence and typhoon exposed.

cities.name: Singapore
cities.blurb: Hyper-connected city-state engineering against heat and sea-level rise; water largely imported.

cities.name: Stockholm
cities.blurb: Cool northern capital with abundant water and low overall hazard, though wildfire summers are emerging.

cities.name: Sydney
cities.blurb: Coastal and highly livable, with bushfire smoke, heatwaves and drought cycles to weigh.

cities.name: Taipei
cities.blurb: High-tech island capital with great healthcare; typhoons, heat and flooding.

cities.name: Tbilisi
cities.blurb: Cheap, low-tax and visa-easy for long stays; smaller global footprint and continental summers.

cities.name: Tel Aviv
cities.blurb: Mediterranean tech hub reliant on desalination; heat, drought and security risk.

cities.name: Tokyo
cities.blurb: Vast, orderly megacity with rising heat and typhoon-driven flood exposure.

cities.name: Toronto
cities.blurb: Great-Lakes water security and mild climate exposure make it a frequent climate-haven pick.

cities.name: Vancouver
cities.blurb: Mild Pacific climate prized as a haven, but heat domes and wildfire smoke now reach it regularly.

cities.name: Vienna
cities.blurb: Perennially high on livability rankings, temperate and water-secure with managed Danube flood risk.

cities.name: Warsaw
cities.blurb: Fast-growing and affordable; continental climate with modest hazard.

cities.name: Zurich
cities.blurb: Cool alpine climate and abundant freshwater make it one of the most climate-insulated cities.
